skill.name: context7
skill.description: Search GitHub issues, pull requests, discussions, and code examples to research dependency bugs and implementation patterns.
skill.summary: Find relevant GitHub issues, PRs, discussions, and examples for dependency research.
skill.author: Dwsy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks and weak cryptography, but review shows these are Markdown examples, inline repository names, and prose. No executable scripts, local command execution, credential handling, prompt injection, or local filesystem access were found. The remaining risk is intended GitHub network search, which can expose repository names and query terms to GitHub.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick patterns are Markdown inline code and fenced search examples. They describe GitHub queries and repository names, not commands that execute locally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious regions are all Markdown formatting or examples. I found no evidence of executable Ruby, shell scripts, or command invocation.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits occur in descriptive text and numbered guidance. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or password handling logic appears in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain ordinary prose about GitHub research. There is no evidence of cryptographic implementation or unsafe algorithm use.

low_findings.title: External GitHub Search Disclosure Risk
low_findings.description: The skill is designed to search GitHub repositories through API or search workflows. Repository names, issue text, and query terms may be sent to GitHub, so users should avoid private secrets in searches.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly describes API-based GitHub searching. This is intended behavior, but it creates a normal third-party query disclosure risk.

content.user_title: Search GitHub Issues and PRs Faster
content.value_statement: Dependency research often requires scanning issues, pull requests, discussions, and examples across multiple repositories. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused GitHub search workflows for faster technical investigation.
actual_capabilities: Guides searches across GitHub issues, pull requests, discussions, and code results.
Suggests repository-specific queries for whisper.cpp, NAudio, WPF, Inno Setup, and .NET runtime.
Provides search operators for labels, dates, authors, reactions, comments, and result locations.
Helps compare closed issues, related pull requests, and release notes for likely fixes.
Organizes research workflows for VoiceLite dependency and implementation questions.
limitations: It does not include a local script or standalone search client.
It depends on available GitHub search or API access in the host assistant.
It may miss private repositories or content hidden by permissions.
Search quality depends on clear repository names, terms, and filters.

use_cases.title: Investigate Dependency Bugs
use_cases.description: Find closed issues, fixes, and related pull requests for errors in external libraries.

use_cases.title: Research Implementation Patterns
use_cases.description: Locate code examples and discussions before adding a feature or changing architecture.

use_cases.title: Prepare Support Triage
use_cases.description: Collect relevant GitHub evidence before deciding whether a defect is local or upstream.

prompt_templates.title: Find Related Issues
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search for one dependency problem.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search GitHub for closed issues about slow transcription in ggerganov/whisper.cpp. Summarize likely fixes and useful links.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Pull Requests
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate research across issues and pull requests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research recent whisper.cpp pull requests about performance optimization. Compare the approaches and note which changes look production ready.

prompt_templates.title: Trace a Bug Across Repositories
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced dependency investigation across related projects.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search NAudio and dotnet/runtime for audio capture disposal problems. Separate upstream library issues from application misuse patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Evidence Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced report for a technical decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research installer missing-file reports in jrsoftware/issrc. Create a decision brief with causes, fixes, confidence, and follow-up searches.

output_examples.input: Search for whisper.cpp performance issues affecting small models.
output_examples.output: A ranked research summary with relevant closed issues, linked pull requests, suspected causes, and recommended next searches.

output_examples.input: Find NAudio memory leak reports involving WaveInEvent.
output_examples.output: A concise triage note covering disposal patterns, recurring mistakes, upstream fixes, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Look for WPF Dispatcher examples for background updates.
output_examples.output: A comparison of useful examples, common threading risks, and search filters that improved result quality.

best_practices: Start with broad terms, then add labels, dates, and result type filters.
Prioritize closed issues and merged pull requests when looking for proven fixes.
Record search terms and rejected results so later reviewers can reproduce the research.
anti_patterns: Do not paste private source code, credentials, or customer data into GitHub searches.
Do not trust one issue comment without checking related pull requests or releases.
Do not search only one repository when a bug may involve a dependency chain.

faq.question: Does this skill execute local commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file contains search guidance and Markdown examples, not executable scripts.

faq.question: What repositories does it focus on?
faq.answer: It highlights whisper.cpp, NAudio, WPF, Inno Setup, and dotnet/runtime, while supporting any GitHub repository.

faq.question: Can it search private repositories?
faq.answer: Only if the host assistant has authorized access through the available GitHub tools or browser session.

faq.question: What information may leave my workspace?
faq.answer: Search terms, repository names, and issue text may be sent to GitHub during normal use.

faq.question: Is it useful outside VoiceLite projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The search methods apply to general dependency research and GitHub issue investigation.

faq.question: Does it guarantee that a GitHub issue contains the correct fix?
faq.answer: No. It helps find evidence, but fixes still need review, testing, and project-specific validation.
